user_message: Pulling in context from Fourth Coffee's exec meeting notes -- need to build the analysis for the renewal risk.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 14:43 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 12:42 EST
Subject: URGENT: renewal risk Issue at Fourth Coffee

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 12:44 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-B trap (Invents replacement-candidate); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-B failure mode (Invents replacement-candidate)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
